articles.summary: Bugs sneak out when less than 80% of user flows are tested before shipping. But getting that kind of coverage—and staying there—is hard and pricey for any size team.

articles.title: SCHEDULE A DEMO TO LEARN MORE
MIGRATING 3.7 MILLION LINES OF FLOW CODE TO TYPESCRIPT
articles.summary: Pinterest successfully migrated 3.7 million lines of code from Flow to TypeScript over eight months. The migration used a "big bang" approach using codemods and had three phases: setup, conversion, and integration. Pinterest validated the migration through detailed testing, including daily automated tests, manual testing, and byte-for-byte comparisons of transpiled JavaScript.

articles.title: MEMOIZING COMPONENTS IN REACT: A CASE FOR USEMEMO
articles.summary: `useMemo` is often a better choice than `React.memo` for optimizing component rendering in React. `useMemo` provides more explicit dependency tracking, makes optimizations visible at the usage site, and has granular control.

articles.title: AI TOOLS ARE IRONICALLY WAY MORE USEFUL FOR EXPERIENCED DEVS THAN NOVICES (REDDIT THREAD)
articles.summary: AI tools are the most useful for experienced devs as a force multiplier. Experienced devs with AI can learn new concepts quicker and overcome knowledge gaps faster, allowing them to work faster with less errors.

articles.title: YOU'RE PROBABLY BREAKING THE LLAMA COMMUNITY LICENSE
articles.summary: Many users and distributors of Meta's Llama models are likely violating the Llama Community License. These violations include failing to prominently display "Built with Llama" on related products/services and not including "Llama" at the beginning of fine-tuned model names.

articles.title: CODEX
articles.summary: The OpenAI Codex CLI is an experimental, open-source coding agent for devs who work in the terminal. It provides ChatGPT-like reasoning with the ability to run code and manipulate files.

articles.title: INTRODUCING OPENAI O3 AND O4-MINI
articles.summary: OpenAI has new models, o3 and o4-mini, that are more intelligent and efficient than previous versions like o1 and o3-mini. These models also have improved safety features, including a rebuilt safety training data and system-level mitigations.

articles.title: ASTRO 5.7
articles.summary: Astro 5.7 has been released. The Experimental Fonts API allows for easy integration and optimization of fonts from various providers. The stable Sessions API allows for secure server-side storage of user data, supporting type-safety and cookie-less options with various storage drivers. SVG components are now natively supported, allowing direct import and use of SVG files as components within Astro projects.

articles.title: TRACT POSTMORTEM
articles.summary: Tract was a UK startup trying to address the housing crisis by improving the planning permission process. It explored various business models, including site sourcing, land appraisal, land promotion, and an AI-powered planning document editor, but struggled with a conservative market, complex land promotion, and low willingness to pay. The founders concluded that the British market's limitations made venture-backed disruption difficult and decided to shut down the company and return capital to investors.

articles.title: OPENAI IN TALKS TO PAY ABOUT $3 BILLION TO ACQUIRE AI CODING STARTUP WINDSURF
articles.summary: OpenAI is reportedly in discussions to acquire Windsurf, an AI-powered code editor with a VSCode extension, for around $3 billion. Windsurf, a competitor to other AI coding tools like Cursor and offerings from Microsoft and Anthropic, is VC-backed and was recently valued at $2.85 billion.

articles.title: PLANDEX
articles.summary: Plandex is an open-source, terminal-based AI coding agent designed for large, real-world projects with features like smart context management, configurable autonomy, and multi-provider model integration.

articles.title: TLS CERTIFICATE LIFETIMES WILL OFFICIALLY REDUCE TO 47 DAYS
articles.summary: The CA/Browser Forum has voted to gradually reduce the maximum lifetime of TLS certificates to 47 days by March 15, 2029.

articles.title: HERB
articles.summary: Herb is an HTML-aware Embedded Ruby parsing tool built on Prism, Ruby's official parser.
